perturbation.original_text: w) "Manufacture," "Manufactured" or "Manufacturing" means all activities involved in the production of Products to be supplied to Customer or its Affiliates hereunder, including the preparation, formulation, finishing, testing, storage and packaging for shipment of Products and the handling, storage and disposal of any residues or wastes generated thereby;
perturbation.changed_text: w) "Manufacture," "Manufactured" or "Manufacturing" means activities involved in the production of Products to be supplied to Customer or its Affiliates hereunder, including the preparation, formulation, and finishing;
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'testing, storage and packaging for shipment of Products and the handling, storage and disposal of any residues or wastes generated thereby' from the definition of 'Manufacture,' the altered text creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5.1 (Release Testing) and Article 7 (Shipment of Product) because these sections explicitly discuss testing and shipment, which are now outside the definition of Manufacturing. This creates uncertainty about who is responsible for these activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) Supplier shall perform release testing of all batches of Product prior to delivery to Customer in accordance with the Product Specifications and the Master Batch Record, to determine whether such batches of Product meet the requirements set out in the Product Specifications. Customer shall be responsible for the final release of Product prior to shipping and further processing.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Manufacture' omits 'testing, storage and packaging for shipment of Products'. The contradicted text, Section 5.1, states 'Supplier shall perform release testing of all batches of Product prior to delivery to Customer.' Because 'testing' is no longer within the definition of 'Manufacture,' this creates a contradiction about the responsibilities of the Supplier, therefore there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: bb) "Product Specifications" means the specifications for the Product set forth in the Quality Agreement, as such may be amended from time to time in accordance with its terms;
perturbation.changed_text: bb) "Product Specifications" means the specifications for the Product outlined in Exhibit A;
perturbation.explanation: The altered definition now directly contradicts any reference to the 'Quality Agreement' in relation to the 'Product Specifications'. This contradicts Section 8.1, which refers to defects or deviations of the Product Specifications. By restricting product specifications to Exhibit A, any specifications outlined in the Quality Agreement are removed, and thus create uncertainty in terms of what specifications the products must follow in Article 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer or its designees shall, within a period of [* * *] after the date of physical receipt of any shipment of Product from Supplier, inspect the Product for any shortages or any defects or deviations of the Product Specifications (hereinafter "Out Of Specification") that would be apparent from visual inspections of the Product. In the event that Customer is of the opinion that the Product is Out Of Specification at the time of delivery, Customer shall, within [* * *] after the date of physical receipt of Product, provide Supplier with a written notice to reject the Product (a "Notice of Rejection"), which shall include a description of the grounds for rejection and copies of test reports and testing methodology conducted on the Product, if any. However, with respect to any Out Of Specification Product which would not be apparent from a reasonable visual inspection on delivery, including in the case of any hidden defects, such Notice of Rejection shall be provided to Supplier not later than [* * *]. The failure of Customer or its designees to notify Supplier of any Out Of Specification Product in the manner set forth herein above shall constitute confirmation of the acceptance thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Product Specifications' now explicitly limits it to Exhibit A, whereas the original definition included the Quality Agreement. Section 8.1 explicitly refers to 'defects or deviations of the Product Specifications,' and by limiting the scope of 'Product Specifications' to Exhibit A, it creates a direct contradiction with this section because defects or deviations outlined in the Quality Agreement would no longer be considered 'Out Of Specification' per the new definition, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: dd) "Recall" means any action by Supplier, Customer or any of their respective Affiliates, to recover possession of the Product or finished products containing the Product shipped to Third Parties. "Recalled" and "Recalling" shall have comparable meanings;
perturbation.changed_text: dd) "Recall" means any action by Supplier, Customer or any of their respective Affiliates, to manage potential safety issues related to the Product. "Recalled" and "Recalling" shall have comparable meanings;
perturbation.explanation: By altering the definition of 'Recall' to focus on 'managing potential safety issues' and removing the specific reference to 'recover possession of the Product...shipped to Third Parties,' the modified text conflicts with the explicit procedures described in Article 17 (Recalls). This changes the meaning of a Recall, now it is only to manage safety issues and removes the need to recover the products, which creates uncertainty about how products shipped to third parties will be recovered in Article 17, which includes the procedures to recover the products from Third Parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party has grounds to recommend a Recall or otherwise receives a notification or information which might result in a Recall, the Party recommending such Recall or receiving such notification or information shall immediately notify the other Party in writing. Subject to Applicable Law, Customer and its designees shall have the sole responsibility to implement any Recall of the Product or any intermediate or finished product containing the Product and the sole right to make all final decisions regarding any such Recall. Supplier shall reasonably cooperate with Customer and its designees in implementing any such Recall, at Customer's expense.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Recall' removes the explicit requirement to 'recover possession of the Product or finished products containing the Product shipped to Third Parties.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the 'Customer and its designees shall have the sole responsibility to implement any Recall of the Product or any intermediate or finished product containing the Product' and that 'Supplier shall reasonably cooperate with Customer...in implementing any such Recall'. By removing the recovery aspect of the recall, the modified definition creates a contradiction regarding how the Customer will implement a Recall, specifically concerning the recovery of products shipped to Third Parties, and the supplier's cooperation in this recovery as outlined in the contradicted text. It introduces uncertainty and creates a strong contradiction, especially regarding the implementation and cooperation aspects of the recall process in Article 17.
